critic_raw: The LSTM+attention predictive maintenance experiment uses a random split on time-to-next-failure (TTNF) time-series data while simultaneously choosing LSTM for its temporal modeling — an internal contradiction. The design also uses only linear regression as baseline for a TTNF task where survival analysis models are more appropriate. Precision at operational thresholds as a regression secondary metric conflates evaluation frameworks.
all_issues_raised: Stratified random split on TTNF time-series contradicts LSTM architecture and creates temporal leakage
Linear regression baseline insufficient — survival analysis models are appropriate for TTNF
Precision at operational thresholds conflates regression and classification evaluation

round_transcripts.critic: Three issues: (1) Random split on TTNF data contradicts LSTM choice — temporal leakage. (2) Linear regression baseline is weak for TTNF where survival analysis (Cox, Weibull) is the natural competitor. (3) Precision at thresholds conflates regression and classification.
round_transcripts.defender: Temporal split is required — TTNF is inherently temporal and the LSTM choice contradicts a cross-sectional split. Survival analysis baseline is more appropriate given censored failure data. Precision-at-threshold is a bridge metric used in maintenance contexts but should be framed as a derived classification metric.

round_transcripts.critic: Defense concedes temporal split needed and baseline weakness. The internal contradiction between LSTM choice and random split is a design validity issue.
round_transcripts.defender: Concede: temporal split required; survival analysis baseline needed; bridge metric should be clearly framed. The design's LSTM choice requires a temporally-sound evaluation.

round_transcripts.critic: Two confirmed design flaws. Design needs temporal split and survival analysis baseline.

all_issues_adjudicated: Temporal leakage from random split contradicts LSTM architecture — temporal split required
Linear regression insufficient baseline for TTNF — survival analysis models needed
